{"id":"bestiary-est","title":"SLICE-5: SQLite v2→v3 migration + QueryByCanonical","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-0ip
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  handoff: bestiary-28c
  depends_on_slice: bestiary-hf4
---

## Specification
Add a v2→v3 schema migration to store.go that renames the existing `family` column to `raw_family` and introduces three new columns (`family`, `variant`, `date`) plus an `idx_canonical` index. Add `QueryByCanonical()` method. Backfill existing rows via the parse package on first migration.

## Files Owned
- store.go (currentSchemaVersion bump 2→3, migrateToV3 mirroring migrateToV2 pattern, scanModelInfo + UpsertModels column-list updates, new QueryByCanonical method)
- store_migration_test.go (5 sub-tests: preserves-data, backfill, idempotent, edge-cases, index-used)
- store_test.go (additions for QueryByCanonical positive/empty/multi-result paths)
- NEW migrations/v2_to_v3.sql (standalone reference SQL — ALTER TABLE / CREATE INDEX statements; this file is documentation, NOT executed by Go code)

## Migration shape (mirror migrateToV2 at store.go:210-286)
1. sqlitex.Transaction(conn)
2. CREATE TABLE models_new with v3 columns: model_id, provider, display_name, raw_family, family, variant, date, … (rest unchanged)
3. INSERT INTO models_new SELECT model_id, provider, display_name, family AS raw_family, '' AS family, '' AS variant, '' AS date, … FROM models
4. Backfill family/variant/date by re-running parse.ParseFamily(raw_family) and parse.ExtractDate(model_id, release_date) over each row.
5. DROP TABLE models; ALTER TABLE models_new RENAME TO models.
6. CREATE INDEX idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider).
7. setSchemaVersion(conn, 3).

## QueryByCanonical signature
```go
// QueryByCanonical returns ModelInfo entries matching the (family, variant, date)
// canonical triple. Cross-provider results are returned as a slice. Empty params
// match any value (e.g., variant="" matches all variants).
func (s *Store) QueryByCanonical(ctx context.Context, family Family, variant, date string) ([]ModelInfo, error)
```

Mirror QueryModels/QueryModelsByID pattern. Use the new `idx_canonical` index. Per CLAUDE.md, ctx is accepted for API consistency but not threaded into SQLite calls (zombiezen/sqlite limitation).

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] currentSchemaVersion = 3
- [ ] migrateToV3 implemented and chained from migrateSchema
- [ ] scanModelInfo reads all v3 columns
- [ ] UpsertModels writes all v3 columns
- [ ] QueryByCanonical returns []ModelInfo
- [ ] idx_canonical exists post-migration (verify via PRAGMA index_list('models'))
- [ ] migrations/v2_to_v3.sql exists and matches Go migration semantically
- [ ] 5 sub-tests pass: preserves-data, backfill, idempotent, edge-cases, index-used

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)
- Given a v2 DB with rows where family="claude-opus" When OpenStore Then v3 DB has raw_family="claude-opus", family="claude", variant="opus", date="" (or extracted date from model_id).
- Given a v3 DB When OpenStore is called twice Then schema version stays at 3 and data is preserved (idempotent).
- Given a v3 DB with multiple rows sharing (family="claude", variant="opus", date="2025-05-14") across providers When QueryByCanonical("claude","opus","2025-05-14") Then returns all matching rows.
- Given v2 → v3 migration When EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN SELECT … WHERE family = ? AND variant = ? AND provider = ? Then plan uses idx_canonical.

## Owner Worker
Wave 3, model: sonnet.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:56:11Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:56:11Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-ktj","title":"SLICE-4: Model_ constants generation (codegen + emitted file)","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-0ip
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  handoff: bestiary-28c
  depends_on_slice: bestiary-hf4
---

## Specification
Add a new codegen step to bestiary-gen that emits per-canonical Model_* constants. Underscore-separated component naming per UAT-1 revision 5. Two-pass collision resolution.

## Files Owned
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go (new generateConstantsSource() + emitter; collision algo)
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go (tests for naming + collision)
- NEW models_constants_gen.go (generated; ~3000+ constants)
- NEW constants_test.go (TestModelConstants_Unique, _RoundTrip, _NamingStable per Reviewer B-F4)

## Naming convention (UAT Option A)
- Format: `Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Version>?_<Date>?` (only Model constants use underscores).
- Within-component characters preserved (`4o` stays `4o`, not `4_o`).
- Version becomes part of name when variant has a numeric trailing version (claude-opus-4 → variant "opus-4" → name segment "Opus_4"; 4.1 collapses to `4_1`? **Worker decision based on test for ModelAnthropicClaudeOpus41 example from PROPOSAL-3 → Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_4_1**).
- Date appended as YYYYMMDD when present.

Examples (must verify generation):
- `Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_4_20250514 ModelID = "claude-opus-4-20250514"`
- `Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_4_1 ModelID = "claude-opus-4-1"`
- `Model_OpenAI_GPT_4o_20240806 ModelID = "gpt-4o-2024-08-06"`
- `Model_Google_Gemini_2_5_Flash_Lite_Preview_20250417 ModelID = "gemini-2.5-flash-lite-preview-04-17"`

## Collision algorithm (two-pass)
Pass 1: Generate naive name. Track collisions.
Pass 2: For each colliding name, append disambiguator in priority order:
  (a) version segment from raw ID (e.g., V41 vs V40)
  (b) sequential `_<n>` suffix (last resort).

## Skip rule
Models with both empty raw family AND no extractable family from ID: skip (no constant generated; raw ID still in registry).

## Public API addition
```go
// Models returns all generated model constants as a defensive copy.
func Models() []ModelID
```

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] models_constants_gen.go exists with all eligible models
- [ ] Names follow Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Date>? pattern
- [ ] Provider/Family/Harness existing PascalCase constants UNTOUCHED (no rename, no breaking change)
- [ ] Two-pass collision resolution produces unique names
- [ ] Models() returns defensive copy (mutating returned slice does not affect future calls)
- [ ] TestModelConstants_Unique, _RoundTrip, _NamingStable pass

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)
- Given two models with the same canonical except date When constants generate Then both produce distinct constants (date disambiguates).
- Given two models that collide on (provider, family, variant) with no version disambiguator When constants generate Then second receives `_2` suffix (or higher).
- Given a model with empty Family and no extractable family When constants generate Then no constant is emitted.
- Given a successful codegen run When Models() is called Then returned slice contains every generated constant value.

## Owner Worker

{"id":"bestiary-qj7","title":"SLICE-3-L5: resolve.go — Resolve + ResolveOption","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-99m
  impl_plan: bestiary-0ip
---
## Scope
Author resolve.go:
- type ResolveOption func(*resolveConfig)
- WithScheme(s CanonicalScheme) ResolveOption — pin scheme; default = auto-detect by prefix.
- Resolve(input string, opts ...ResolveOption) ([]ModelRef, error) — implements URD R6 / Reviewer C-N1 contract.

Resolve algorithm:
1. Detect or apply explicit Scheme.
2. Match against StaticModels() (and SQLite cache if available).
3. Group matches by Canonical = (NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, NormalizedDate).
4. If 0 matches: return ErrNotFound{What:"model", Key:input}.
5. If matches share 1 distinct Canonical: return []ModelRef from each match's Ref(), err=nil (cross-provider hosting).
6. If matches span >= 2 distinct Canonicals: return *ErrAmbiguous with candidate list, err = &ErrAmbiguous{...}.

Author resolve_test.go covering all four code paths plus auto-detect by prefix.

## Files Owned
- resolve.go
- resolve_test.go

Documentation slice. Updates JSON Schema for the new public type shapes, bumps the schema version to 0.0.2 (NOT 0.1.0), and authors the migration document covering all changes from v0.0.1 → v0.0.2.

7. SQLite v2→v3 column renames: family→raw_family, NEW family/variant/date columns + idx_canonical index. Migration is automatic on first OpenStore call.
8. NEW Model_* constants (underscore-separated). Provider/Family/Harness UNCHANGED (PascalCase preserved — no breaking change).

- Given the migration doc When read by a downstream consumer (provenance) Then they can identify every breaking and non-breaking change with file/line specificity.

{"id":"bestiary-2i0","title":"SLICE-6a: bestiary show --scheme + ErrAmbiguous rendering","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-0ip
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  handoff: bestiary-28c
  depends_on_slice: bestiary-99m
---

## Specification
Two orthogonal CLI changes bundled in one slice (single owner of cmd/* paths to avoid contention):

A. `bestiary show <input> [--scheme=<canonical|huggingface|purl|raw>]` — auto-detects scheme by prefix when flag absent. Resolves via Resolve(). Prints candidate table on *ErrAmbiguous.

B. `bestiary-gen [--cache-dir <path>] [--no-fetch]` — overrides hardcoded `.bestiary-gen-cache/`; `--no-fetch` reads cached api_response.json instead of HTTP. Cache miss with --no-fetch → actionable error per C-actionable-errors.

## Files Owned
- cmd/bestiary/main.go (add --scheme flag to show subcommand; replace direct LookupModel call with Resolve; render *ErrAmbiguous candidate table)
- cmd/bestiary/main_test.go (scheme tests; ambiguity rendering test)
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go (parseFlags additions; cache path threading; --no-fetch read-cache path)
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go (cache flag tests)
- format.go (helper for rendering ErrAmbiguous candidate table — reuse tableRow pattern)

## Auto-detect rules
| Input prefix | Scheme |
|---|---|
| `pkg:huggingface/` | SchemePURL |
| Contains `/` (and no `pkg:`) | SchemeHuggingFace |
| Default | SchemeCanonical (which falls through to raw lookup if no canonical match) |
| Last-resort fall-through | SchemeRaw — direct ModelID lookup |

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] `bestiary show claude-opus-4-20250514` returns the model.
- [ ] `bestiary show "anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514"` (HuggingFace form) resolves.
- [ ] `bestiary show "pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514"` (PURL) resolves.
- [ ] `bestiary show claude --scheme=canonical` returns *ErrAmbiguous with Opus/Sonnet/Haiku candidates listed.
- [ ] `bestiary-gen --cache-dir /tmp/foo` writes to /tmp/foo/api_response.json.
- [ ] `bestiary-gen --no-fetch` with stale cache succeeds; with missing cache returns actionable error containing path + remediation.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)
- Given input "claude" When `bestiary show claude` Then exit code != 0 and stdout/stderr lists candidates from *ErrAmbiguous.
- Given input "pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514" When `bestiary show` Then output is the resolved model.
- Given a missing cache file When `bestiary-gen --no-fetch --cache-dir /nonexistent` Then exit != 0 and message tells the user (a) what file was expected, (b) why fetch was skipped (--no-fetch), (c) how to fix (drop --no-fetch or place a cached api_response.json at the path).

## Owner Worker

{"id":"bestiary-0ip","title":"IMPL_PLAN: Entity normalization pipeline","description":"---
references:
  request: bestiary-n6x
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  elicit: bestiary-73x
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  handoff: bestiary-28c
  uat: bestiary-qz7
---

## Objective
Decompose the ratified PROPOSAL-3 (bestiary-1oq) into vertical slices with explicit horizontal Layer Integration Points. Each slice owns a full vertical (types → tests → impl → wiring) and is implemented by exactly one worker.

## Decomposition: 8 Vertical Slices

### Slice 1 — parse package + codegen template apply (foundation)
Owner files (NEW): `parse.go`, `parse_test.go`, `parse/data/family_overrides.json`, `parse/data/variant_suffixes.json`, `parse/data/version_patterns.json`.
Touched: `families_gen.go`, `models_static_gen.go`, `providers_gen.go` (regenerate via `go generate ./...` to apply commit 6a8d75b template fix — must end with `type Family string`, NOT `type Family = string`). `.bestiary-gen-cache/api_response.json` (dirty, will be re-cached).

Deliverables:
- `ParseFamily(raw Family) (Family, string)` — uses overrides table → suffix table → versioned-variant patterns (v/k/m/no-prefix). Hyphen-separated versions (claude-opus-4-5) handled. Empty raw families return zero values.
- `ExtractDate(id ModelID, releaseDate string) string` — match YYYYMMDD or YYYY-MM-DD only.
- `inferFamilyFromID(id ModelID, p Provider) Family` — fallback for empty-family models (~25%).
- Embed JSON via `//go:embed parse/data/*.json` (regular package directory, NOT testdata/).
- `go generate ./...` regenerates all `_gen.go` files with named Family type.

### Slice 2 — ModelInfo extension + family methods + codegen normalization splice
Owner files (NEW): `family.go`, `family_test.go`.
Touched: `bestiary.go` (ModelInfo new fields), `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (genToModelInfo at line 524, validateGeneratedFamilyType post-condition).

Deliverables:
- ModelInfo gains `NormalizedFamily Family`, `NormalizedVariant string`, `NormalizedDate string`.
- `family.go`: `Family.IsKnown()`, `String()`, `MarshalText()`, `UnmarshalText()` — resolves debt task bestiary-ptm.
- Codegen splice: invoke `parse.ParseFamily` + `parse.ExtractDate` in `genToModelInfo()` to bake Normalized fields into `models_static_gen.go`.
- `validateGeneratedFamilyType(path)` post-condition runs at end of `run()` and asserts grep for `type Family string` (no `=`).
- TestFamilyType_NamedNotAlias regression test in package root.

### Slice 3 — ModelRef + canonical + designation + Resolve (public API)
Owner files (NEW): `canonical.go`, `designation.go`, `resolve.go`, `canonical_test.go`, `designation_test.go`, `resolve_test.go`.
Touched: `modelref.go` (ModelRef gains ID field, becomes 6-field; Format(scheme) dispatch; Designations() method; Stringer uses SchemeCanonical), `errors.go` (NEW *ErrAmbiguous struct error), `errors_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- `CanonicalScheme` int enum: SchemeCanonical | SchemeHuggingFace | SchemePURL | SchemeRaw (UAT Option A formats).
- `Designation{Value, Scheme, Provider, Rating}`, `AcceptabilityRating` int enum (Admitted | Preferred | Deprecated). All static designations default to Admitted.
- ModelRef 6 fields: ID, Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Date.
- ModelRef.Format(scheme), ModelRef.Designations().
- `Resolve(input string, opts ...ResolveOption) ([]ModelRef, error)` — slice for cross-provider hosting, *ErrAmbiguous for distinct canonicals, ErrNotFound for zero. WithScheme(s) option.

### Slice 4 — Model_ constants generation

{"id":"bestiary-2jqs","title":"IMPORTANT: ExtractDate accepts semantically invalid dates (e.g., 9999-99-99)","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-n2q
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-czu0
---
## Finding

ExtractDate uses two regexes to match YYYYMMDD and YYYY-MM-DD patterns. The patterns correctly match the structural form but do not validate that month (MM) is 01-12 or day (DD) is 01-31. A model ID like 'model-9999-99-99' returns '9999-99-99' without error or truncation.

## Impact

Low in this epoch: real model IDs from models.dev use valid dates. This could produce nonsensical output if the upstream API ever returns malformed date strings.

## Suggestion

Add a parse-time validation step in extractDateFromString to verify MM ∈ [01,12] and DD ∈ [01,31]. Or document explicitly that date validation is out-of-scope for this epoch (acceptable for follow-up). This is not a blocker but should be tracked.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:19:58Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:19:58Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-bzdy","title":"IMPORTANT: sync.Once error path is silently swallowed and untestable","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-n2q
  reviewer: reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
## Finding

`loadParseData()` uses `sync.Once` with package-level globals (`parseOnce`, `parsed`, `parseErr`). When `initParseData()` fails (e.g., an invalid regex in `version_patterns.json`), the error is cached permanently and `ParseFamily` silently degrades to returning the raw value unchanged.

## Why it matters

1. **Untestable**: The error path in `initParseData` cannot be exercised by any test — `sync.Once` fires once per process, making it impossible to inject a bad regex or missing file in a unit test.
2. **Silent failure**: `ParseFamily(raw Family) (Family, string)` returns no error. If startup fails, callers receive `(raw, "")` with no diagnostic signal. For codegen this means silently emitting un-normalized data into `models_static_gen.go` for all ~110 models.
3. **No test coverage**: There is no test for the malformed-regex or missing-embed error paths.

## Suggested fix

Option A (minimal): Add a `TestParseData_RegexesValid` test that calls `loadParseData()` and asserts the error is nil, plus documents the fail-closed behavior explicitly in the godoc (it currently says "This should only happen in pathological build configurations" — accurate, but doesn't say it's silent).

Option B (stronger): Expose `initParseData` as a testable function (already done — it's package-private). Add a test that constructs a `versionPattern` with a bad regex and calls `regexp.Compile` directly to document the behavior, without needing to mutate globals. This is already achievable; it just hasn't been done.

Option C: Convert `ParseFamily` to `ParseFamily(raw Family) (Family, string, error)`. This is an API break relative to the slice spec and the proposal — PROPOSAL-3 ratified the 2-return signature. Not recommended unless the review round decides the silent degradation is unacceptable for a data integrity use case.

The fail-closed choice is reasonable for a production binary (embedded data cannot go missing), but the lack of a test that even exercises `loadParseData()` success leaves a coverage gap.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:28Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:28Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-31v2","title":"MINOR: TestParseFamily_VersionedPatterns comment incorrectly says minimax-m2.5 is not in overrides","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-n2q
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-ia6l

---
## Finding

In TestParseFamily_VersionedPatterns, the test case for 'minimax-m2.5' has the description 'minimax-m2.5 (not in overrides, fallthrough to pattern)'. This comment is factually incorrect: minimax-m2.5 IS present in parse/data/family_overrides.json (line confirms: { "family": "minimax", "variant": "m2.5" }).

The test result is correct regardless — overrides and the m-prefix pattern agree on the output ('minimax', 'm2.5'). But the misleading comment could confuse future maintainers adding or removing override entries.

## Suggestion

Change the test case comment to: 'minimax-m2.5 (via overrides, but pattern also matches — same result)' OR move this case to TestParseFamily_Overrides. Better yet, add a truly pattern-only m-prefix test case (e.g. 'someai-m3.0') to specifically exercise the pattern path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:20:12Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:20:12Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-0gtu","title":"MINOR: variant_suffixes.json _comment misleads about ordering being authoritative","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-n2q
  reviewer: reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
## Finding

`parse/data/variant_suffixes.json` contains this `_comment`:

> "Longest entries must come first to avoid premature short-match."

However, `initParseData()` re-sorts the suffixes by length at runtime (`sort.Slice` by `len`). The JSON ordering is therefore **irrelevant to correctness** — the runtime sort is the authority.

This misleads future data file editors: they may spend time maintaining the "correct" JSON order when it does not matter, or they may trust that JSON order controls behavior and be surprised when it doesn't.

## Suggested fix

Update the `_comment` to:

> "List of variant suffix patterns (with leading hyphen). Order in this file does not affect runtime behavior — initParseData sorts suffixes longest-first automatically."

This is a documentation-only fix with no code change.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:41Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:41Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-h5wr","title":"MINOR: parse/ directory naming ambiguity (looks like a Go subpackage)","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-n2q
  reviewer: reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
## Finding

The directory `parse/data/` sits under a directory named `parse/`. In Go convention, a directory containing `.go` files is a package. While `parse/` currently contains no `.go` files (data assets only), a future contributor might reasonably assume it could become a sub-package (consistent with the PROPOSAL-2 → PROPOSAL-3 rename from `decompose/` to `parse/`). The slice spec itself says "Worker decision: place at root package; the dir parse/data/ is just a data-asset dir."

This is not a correctness issue. The code compiles and works. The `go:embed` path `parse/data/*.json` is valid. The concern is that the directory name `parse` could invite placing Go source files there in a future slice, creating an import-cycle risk (since the root `bestiary` package embeds from `parse/data/`).

## Suggested mitigation

Two options, either acceptable:

1. **No change needed if future slices stay away.** The naming was ratified via UAT-1 (RENAME from `decompose` → `parse`). Document in `parse/data/README` (or a `doc.go` with build tag exclusion) that this directory is data-only.
2. **Rename to `_parse/data/`** — Go tool ignores directories starting with `_`. But this conflicts with `go:embed` rules (embed cannot embed from `_` directories).

Option 1 is preferred. The `parse/data/` naming decision was explicitly ratified and is correct for `go:embed`. This finding is informational.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:54Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:54Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-ao7n","title":"MINOR: TestProvider_UnmarshalUnknownAccepted stored-value assertion is vacuous — all test inputs are already lowercase","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-8yfq
  leaf: bestiary-am6s
  reviewer: reviewer-B
  round: 1
---
## Finding

`TestProvider_UnmarshalUnknownAccepted` asserts after UnmarshalText that:

```go
if string(p) != s {
    t.Errorf("UnmarshalText(%q) stored %q, want %q", s, string(p), s)
}
```

The comment says 'Value must be stored (lowercased)'. But all 6 test inputs ("amazon-bedrock", "completely-unknown-vendor", "mistral", "cohere", "xai", "vertex-ai") are already fully lowercase. `strings.ToLower` applied to them is a no-op, so the assertion trivially passes even if someone removed the `strings.ToLower` call from `UnmarshalText`.

## Why it matters

The test does not actually verify the lowercasing behavior — it only verifies that the string is stored at all. If UnmarshalText were changed to store as-is (no lowercasing), the test would still pass. This is asserting internal state behavior without actually exercising the interesting case.

## Fix

Add at least one mixed-case input to the test list, e.g.:

```go
unknowns := []struct{ input, want string }{
    {"amazon-bedrock", "amazon-bedrock"},
    {"COMPLETELY-UNKNOWN", "completely-unknown"},   // exercises lowercasing
    {"Mistral", "mistral"},                          // exercises lowercasing
    {"cohere", "cohere"},
}
```

This would actually catch a regression where lowercasing was removed.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:13:23Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:13:23Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-3anm","title":"MINOR: No test for MarshalText with empty Provider — previously a validation error, now silently marshals to empty bytes","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-8yfq
  reviewer: reviewer-A-correctness
  round: 1
  commit: 5ce800e
  file: pkg/ptypes/enums.go
---
## Finding

Before the refactor, MarshalText rejected invalid Providers with an error:
  if !p.IsValid() { return nil, fmt.Errorf("...") }

After the refactor, MarshalText is unconditional:
  return []byte(p), nil

This means Provider("").MarshalText() now returns ([]byte(""), nil) — no error. This is correct per the spec ('permissive'), but there is no test covering this behavior. The existing TestProviderRoundTrip does not include an empty-string case.

The silent empty-byte marshal is technically correct since IsValid() guards empty strings. But a test asserting the behavior change (empty marshals to empty bytes without error) would prevent future regression.

Fix: Add a case to TestProviderRoundTrip or a standalone test:
  b, err := Provider("").MarshalText()
  if err != nil { t.Errorf(...) }
  if string(b) != "" { t.Errorf(...) }","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:13:04Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:13:04Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-6i66","title":"MINOR: TestProvider_UnmarshalUnknownAccepted has latent gap — all test inputs are lowercase but assertion compares against non-lowercased input","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-8yfq
  reviewer: reviewer-A-correctness
  round: 1
  commit: 95017f8
  file: pkg/ptypes/enums_test.go
---
## Finding

The test TestProvider_UnmarshalUnknownAccepted asserts:
  if string(p) != s { t.Errorf(...) }

where s is the original input string and UnmarshalText lowercases before storing.

All 6 test inputs happen to be already-lowercase ('amazon-bedrock', 'completely-unknown-vendor', etc.) so the assertion passes. But if a future maintainer adds a mixed-case input like 'Amazon-Bedrock', the assertion would fail incorrectly (stored as 'amazon-bedrock', compared against 'Amazon-Bedrock') — even though the code is correct.

Fix: Change assertion to compare against strings.ToLower(s):
  if string(p) != strings.ToLower(s) { t.Errorf(...) }

The comment '// Value must be stored (lowercased)' already signals the intent; the assertion just needs to match it.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:12:50Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:12:50Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-4udi","title":"MINOR: IsValid rejects whitespace-only strings but UnmarshalText does not","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-8yfq
  reviewer: Reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
## Finding

IsValid() uses TrimSpace to reject whitespace-only strings (e.g., '   ' returns false).
UnmarshalText() does NOT strip or reject whitespace — it lowercases and stores the value as-is.

This creates a subtle contract asymmetry:
  UnmarshalText('   ') → stores Provider('   '), no error
  Provider('   ').IsValid() → false

So a round-trip of UnmarshalText + IsValid fails for whitespace-only input, even though MarshalText + UnmarshalText alone succeeds. This is not caught by any test.

The TestProviderRoundTrip test only tests non-whitespace values. The whitespace-only case in TestProviderIsValid only tests IsValid in isolation, not the combined path.

## Severity rationale

This is a minor inconsistency — whitespace-only provider strings are pathological inputs. The real-world impact is near-zero. But the asymmetry is surprising and not documented.

## Fix suggestion

Either: (a) have UnmarshalText strip whitespace before storing (TrimSpace + lowercase), or (b) document the asymmetry explicitly in the IsValid doc comment ('Note: whitespace-only strings pass UnmarshalText but fail IsValid'). Option (a) is cleaner.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:12:07Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:12:07Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-hhdj","title":"MINOR: TestValidateGeneratedFamilyType alias-form case comment is incorrect","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go
  commit: 2a0b715
---
## Finding

The test case for alias-form detection ("failing: alias form present") has this comment:
```
// Both conditions fire: alias is present; but first we check for
// the named declaration absence. The alias form does NOT contain
// "type Family string" (without "="), so the first check fires.
```

The comment says "both conditions fire" but that is wrong. Only the FIRST condition fires (namedDecl not found). The second condition (aliasDecl found) can only fire if both the named form AND the alias form are present simultaneously, which is not the case in this test. The test is correct, only the explanatory comment is misleading.

Verified: bytes.Contains(b"type Family = string", b"type Family string") == False.

## Fix suggestion

Replace "Both conditions fire" with "Only the first condition fires":
```go
// Only the first condition fires: the alias form ("type Family = string") does NOT
// contain the named-type declaration string ("type Family string", without "="),
// so the namedDecl check reports the missing declaration.
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:42:33Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:42:33Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-hcab","title":"IMPORTANT: empty-family InferFamilyFromID path not exercised in codegen tests (~25% of models)","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-B
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-oju4
---
## Finding

The codegen splice comment explicitly notes '~25% of models have an empty Family field — infer from the model ID'. InferFamilyFromID is covered in parse_test.go (TestInferFamilyFromID), but there is no test in cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go that feeds a model with an empty Family field through genToModelInfo (or a mock run()) and asserts that NormalizedFamily is populated from InferFamilyFromID.

The empty-family branch in genToModelInfo is:
  if rawFamily != '' {
      normFamily, normVariant = ParseFamily(rawFamily)
  } else {
      normFamily = InferFamilyFromID(id, provider)  // <-- this branch is untested at the codegen layer
  }

If this branch silently broke (e.g. due to a future refactor), there would be no codegen-layer test failure.

## Why IMPORTANT (not BLOCKER)
Unit-level coverage exists in parse_test.go for InferFamilyFromID. The codegen integration of that function is the untested path. Code correct today; risk is silent regression.

## Suggested fix
Add a case in makeTestModels() or a new test using minimalAPIJSON with a model that has 'family': '' (omitted), then assert NormalizedFamily is non-empty in the generated output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:39:31Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:39:31Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-olmz","title":"IMPORTANT: no integration test asserting normalized fields on generated models (end-to-end splice path untested)","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-B
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-oju4
---
## Finding

TestCacheDirFlag exercises the full run() code path end-to-end (real HTTP mock → codegen → file write), but the test only asserts that api_response.json was written to the correct directory. It never reads back models_static_gen.go and checks that NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date are non-zero for any model.

Likewise, TestNoFetch_HitsCache verifies that the cache is read and models/provMeta are returned, but makes no assertions on the content of the generated file.

The Worker's commit message claims '3 sample models work' and the lm1 task comment has a manual spot-check, but there is no automated assertion proving that:
- claude-opus-4-20250514 → NormalizedFamily='claude', NormalizedVariant='opus', NormalizedDate='2025-05-14'
- gpt-4o-2024-08-06 → NormalizedFamily='gpt', NormalizedVariant='', NormalizedDate='2024-08-06'

This is the core splice path. If someone breaks genToModelInfo or the template in generateSource, the only guard currently is that go build still compiles. The regression would be silent.

## Why IMPORTANT (not BLOCKER)
Tests pass; the code is correct as of now. The risk is future regressions go undetected.

## Suggested fix
Extend TestCacheDirFlag or add a new TestGenToModelInfo_NormalizedFields test that:
1. Constructs a minimalAPIJSON with known family/ID fields.
2. Calls run() or genToModelInfo() directly.
3. Asserts NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date on the returned ModelInfo (or in the written file content).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:39:18Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:39:18Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-ee6f","title":"MINOR: --cache-dir= (empty value) silently sets cacheDir to empty string","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-a1t2
---
## Finding

**File:** cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go, lines 252-253

`case strings.HasPrefix(arg, "-cache-dir="): res.cacheDir = strings.TrimPrefix(arg, "-cache-dir=")`

If the user passes `-cache-dir=` (equals sign, empty value), `res.cacheDir` is set to `""`. Subsequent calls to `cacheAPIResponse("", ...)` and `fetchModelsWithRaw(ctx, "", ...)` will attempt `filepath.Join("", cacheFile)` which resolves to just `api_response.json` in the current working directory — a silent misbehavior.

Similarly, `-cache-dir` as the last argument (no following token) is silently ignored and falls back to `defaultCacheDir` — that's correct behavior but is undocumented.

**What:** Empty `--cache-dir` value produces an unintended working-directory cache path.
**Why:** No validation that the parsed `cacheDir` is non-empty.
**Where:** parseFlags(), lines 252-253.
**Impact:** Minor — unusual invocation. No data loss. User would see a confusing file appearing in cwd.

## How to fix

After parsing, validate: if `res.cacheDir == ""`, return an error: `--cache-dir value must not be empty`. Or treat empty as defaultCacheDir.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:58Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:58Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-6d4w","title":"IMPORTANT: TestNoFetch_MissingCache_ActionableError verifies only 3 of 6 C-actionable-errors fields","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 1
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go
  lines: 356-386
  constraint: C-actionable-errors
---
## Finding

ErrCacheMiss.Error() emits all six C-actionable-errors fields (What, Why, Where, When, What-it-means, How-to-fix). The test only asserts three of them:

  - absWant path (covers Where)
  - '--no-fetch' substring (covers Why, partially)
  - 're-run without --no-fetch' (covers How-to-fix)

Unchecked in the test:
  - 'What': 'cached api_response.json missing or empty'
  - 'When': 'during cache load step in fetchModelsWithRaw'
  - 'What it means': 'bestiary-gen cannot proceed without API data'

If a future refactor removes or misspells these fields from ErrCacheMiss.Error(), the test will not catch it. The constraint C-actionable-errors is a first-class protocol requirement for all six fields.

## How to fix

Add three more assertions after the existing ones:
    if !strings.Contains(msg, 'cached api_response.json missing') {
        t.Errorf('error message missing What field')
    }
    if !strings.Contains(msg, 'cannot proceed without API data') {
        t.Errorf('error message missing What-it-means field')
    }
    if !strings.Contains(msg, 'fetchModelsWithRaw') {
        t.Errorf('error message missing When/Where field')
    }","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:39Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:39Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-w887","title":"MINOR: No negative-path tests for write-permission error or malformed JSON in cache","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 1
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go
---
## Finding

The spec (bestiary-f1f3) defines three test cases: TestCacheDirFlag, TestNoFetch_HitsCache, TestNoFetch_MissingCache_ActionableError. These are all implemented. However, two negative-path scenarios that would exercise real error handling are absent:

1. **Write-permission denied to cache dir**: cacheAPIResponse calls os.MkdirAll then os.WriteFile. A test with a read-only dir (os.Chmod(tmpDir, 0o444)) would confirm the warning path in run() ('bestiary-gen: warning: could not cache API response') actually fires and does not panic. Only applies to Linux/macOS where chmod is meaningful; can be guarded with t.Skipf on Windows.

2. **Malformed JSON in cache with --no-fetch**: If api_response.json exists but contains invalid JSON, fetchModelsWithRaw returns raw bytes successfully (os.ReadFile succeeds) but the subsequent json.Unmarshal in parseAPIResponse fails. The test suite has no coverage of this path, leaving the error message for corrupt caches untested.

## Impact

Low — these are edge cases not in the MVP spec. The happy-path coverage is complete for the three spec-required tests.

## How to fix

Optional follow-up:
    func TestCacheAPIResponse_PermissionDenied(t *testing.T) {
        if os.Getuid() == 0 { t.Skip('running as root; chmod has no effect') }
        dir := t.TempDir()
        os.Chmod(dir, 0o444)
        defer os.Chmod(dir, 0o755)
        err := cacheAPIResponse([]byte{'{}'), dir)
        if err == nil { t.Error('expected error for unwritable dir, got nil') }
    }","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:19:10Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:19:10Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-yzl6","title":"MINOR: TestCacheDirFlag does not actually verify --cache-dir prevents writes to defaultCacheDir","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: C
  round: 1
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go
  lines: "270-321"
---
## Finding

TestCacheDirFlag contains this comment and intentional no-op:

  // The default path may already exist (repo has a cached file);
  // compare mod times or just confirm tmpDir file exists — the key
  // assertion is that cacheAPIResponse honoured the dir argument.
  // So this branch is intentionally a no-op (not an error).
  _ = defaultPath

The test documents WHY it punts (the repo cache already exists), but by doing so it only half-verifies the contract. The validation checklist item says 'bestiary-gen --cache-dir /tmp/foo writes to /tmp/foo/api_response.json' — the test confirms the write to tmpDir but silently skips confirming the default cache dir was NOT newly touched.

## Impact

If a future refactor reintroduces writing to defaultCacheDir regardless of the --cache-dir flag, this test will not catch the regression.

## Suggestion

Use t.TempDir() for BOTH the target dir AND a substitute defaultCacheDir (swap via a package-level var or test helper), so the 'no side-effect on default dir' assertion becomes testable. Alternatively, capture the mtime of defaultCacheDir before the call and assert it is unchanged after.

This is MINOR because the primary assertion (writes to tmpDir) is correct and the test passes. The gap is in the negative assertion only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:21Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:21Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-2mli","title":"MINOR: contacted flag in TestNoFetch_HitsCache cannot trip via apiURL override on noFetch=true path","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 2
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go
  lines: 350-361
---
## Finding

TestNoFetch_HitsCache now wires apiURL = srv.URL (correctly fixing the cycle-1 IMPORTANT bestiary-szxm). However, when fetchModelsWithRaw is called with noFetch=true, the noFetch branch reads from disk via os.ReadFile and returns before ever reaching the 'http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, apiURL, ...)' call. Therefore apiURL is never read in this code path and contacted can never be set to true regardless of what apiURL is overridden to.

The guard is structurally sound: if a future refactor removes the noFetch guard and makes an HTTP call, apiURL=srv.URL does ensure it would hit the guard server. But the current test narrative ('any accidental HTTP fetch would hit our guard server') is slightly aspirational — it only works if the regression is of the form 'calls apiURL directly'. A regression that uses a hardcoded URL or a separate http.Get would bypass it.

This is a documentation gap more than a code gap. The test is correct and the contacted=false assertion is valid.

## How to fix (optional follow-up)

A comment clarifying the residual limitation would be sufficient:
  // Note: contacted trips only if a regression calls apiURL. A hardcoded URL regression would not be caught.

Not required for acceptance.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:27:55Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:27:55Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-1kkb","title":"MINOR: ErrCacheMiss.Error() duplicates the 'What' sentence as both the leading summary and a labelled field","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ektc
  reviewer: C
  round: 1
  file: cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go
  lines: "449-460"
---
## Finding

ErrCacheMiss.Error() opens with:

  'cached api_response.json missing or empty\n  What: cached api_response.json missing or empty\n'

The first line and the 'What:' label are identical. For a user reading the error in a terminal, this reads as a stutter: the same sentence appears twice in two consecutive lines.

## Impact

Minor readability regression. The C-actionable-errors template asks for the summary first (that's the leading line), then the structured labels. The leading line IS the 'What', so repeating it as a labelled field is redundant.

## Suggestion

Either:
  (a) remove 'What: ...' from the labelled list (the first line already serves that role), OR
  (b) make the leading line a short one-liner (e.g. 'cache miss: %s') and use 'What: cached api_response.json missing or empty' as the structured label.

Option (a) is simpler and consistent with how the other fmt.Errorf messages in this file handle it (they have a leading summary followed by specific structured labels that add NEW information).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:08Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:18:08Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-gx2b","title":"MINOR: extractVersionSegment tokenizes family/variant with old hyphen-dot splitter, rawID with new non-alphanumeric splitter — undocumented asymmetry","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ktj
  reviewer: reviewer-C
  round: 1
  leaf_of: bestiary-f3ni
---
## Finding

extractVersionSegment (cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go ~line 1329) tokenizes the rawID with the new universal splitter:
  return !unicode.IsLetter(r) && !unicode.IsDigit(r)

But it tokenizes familyTokens and variantTokens with the old narrow splitter:
  return r == '-' || r == '.'

In practice NormalizedFamily and NormalizedVariant only ever contain hyphens and dots as separators, so the mismatch is benign today. However, if a future provider introduces underscores or colons in a normalized family slug, the knownTokens set would fail to match the rawID tokens, causing more of the version segment to survive (noisier version suffix), and more chance of collision disambiguation failing.

## Why MINOR (not IMPORTANT)

The practical impact is zero today: NormalizedFamily comes from models.dev's normalized fields which historically use only hyphens. The risk is theoretical.

## How to fix

Unify the tokenizer: extract a package-level splitFunc and reuse it in both callsites. Adds one line of shared code, removes the cognitive overhead of 'which splitter are we using here?'","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:14:40Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:14:40Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-cave","title":"MINOR: defensive-copy check duplicated across NamingStable and DefensiveCopy tests","description":"---

---

TestModelConstants_NamingStable (constants_test.go lines 87-93) performs a mutation + check for defensive copy, then TestModelIDs_DefensiveCopy (lines 115-131) repeats the exact same check. The duplication dilutes both tests and makes it unclear which one owns the behaviour.

## Suggested Fix

Remove the defensive-copy block from TestModelConstants_NamingStable, have it focus only on value-format assertions (values are raw IDs, not Go identifiers). Leave TestModelIDs_DefensiveCopy as the sole owner of mutation safety.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:12:56Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:12:56Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-efus","title":"MINOR: TestResolveCollisions_VersionSuffix does not assert which result contains the version token","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ktj
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 1
---
## Finding

TestResolveCollisions_VersionSuffix (main_test.go ~line 400) checks that "4" appears somewhere in resolved[0] OR resolved[1] via:

    if !strings.Contains(resolved[0], "4") && !strings.Contains(resolved[1], "4") {

The digit "4" is common enough in provider/family tokens that this check could pass vacuously without the version-suffix logic actually firing. It also doesn't verify that "3" or "3_5" appears in the other result.

    want0, want1 := "Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_4", "Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_3_5"
    if (resolved[0] != want0 || resolved[1] != want1) && (resolved[0] != want1 || resolved[1] != want0) {

{"id":"bestiary-o19g","title":"MINOR: TestModelConstants_NamingStable does not test determinism as spec requires","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ktj
  leaf_task: bestiary-2u5
  reviewer: reviewer-A-correctness
  round: 1
  severity: minor
---
## Finding

PROPOSAL-3 spec (bestiary-2u5) and PROPOSAL-3 deliverable text describe:
`TestModelConstants_NamingStable: re-run codegen via 'go test -tags=codegen-stability' or use a recorded fixture; assert deterministic naming.`

Worker's implementation tests:
1. Defensive copy (mutation of returned slice does not affect next call)
2. Values do not start with "Model_" (they're raw API IDs)

The actual DETERMINISM check (re-run codegen twice and compare constant names) is NOT implemented.

## Impact

Low: The codegen IS deterministic (uses `sort.Slice` by constant name in `generateConstantsSource`), and the sort is verified transitively by `TestGenerateConstantsSource_Compiles`. The determinism test would be an integration-level test requiring network or a large fixture, which explains the worker's omission.

The test name "NamingStable" implies a property the body doesn't actually exercise.

## Suggestion

Either rename the test to reflect what it actually tests (e.g. `TestModelIDs_Values`) and create a separate `TestModelConstants_Deterministic` that calls `generateConstantsSource` twice with the same input and asserts identical output, OR add a note in the test doc comment explaining why determinism is guaranteed statically (sort.Slice) and not tested at runtime.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:14:45Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:14:45Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-bbqy","title":"MINOR: alias-form test case errFrag comment is misleading (fires wrong check, passes for wrong reason)","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-B
  round: 1
  severity_group: bestiary-aot2
---
## Finding

In TestValidateGeneratedFamilyType, the 'failing: alias form present' case has this comment:
  // Both conditions fire: alias is present; but first we check for
  // the named declaration absence. The alias form does NOT contain
  // 'type Family string' (without '='), so the first check fires.
  errFrag: 'named-type declaration not found',

The comment acknowledges the test is exercising the first check (missing named-type), NOT the second check (alias present). This means the second code path in validateGeneratedFamilyType:
  if bytes.Contains(src, aliasDecl) { return fmt.Errorf(...'alias declaration found'...) }
is never exercised by any test case.

A synthetic file that contains BOTH 'type Family string' AND 'type Family = string' would reach the alias-found branch. No such case exists.

This is a minor gap: the alias-detection branch is dead from a test perspective. If someone removed the alias check entirely, no test would fail.

## Why MINOR
The function works correctly; the risk is cosmetic and the alias-detection branch being untested is unlikely to matter in practice given the strong named-type assertion runs first.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:40:02Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:40:02Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-52d3","title":"SLICE6a-REVIEW-A-1-MINOR: QueryModels used for cross-provider ref lookup; may be slow at scale","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T18:39:48Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T18:39:48Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-seps","title":"IMPORTANT: idx_canonical not created for fresh v3 databases (schemaSQL gap)","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
---
## Finding

store.go's `schemaSQL` constant creates the v3 `models` table with all four columns (raw_family, family, variant, date) but does NOT include a `CREATE INDEX` statement for `idx_canonical`.

The index is only created inside `migrateToV3()`, which is only called for databases that are upgrading from v1/v2. A fresh installation (first `OpenStore` with no prior DB) follows the `!hasModels` branch in `migrateSchema()`, which executes `schemaSQL` and then calls `setSchemaVersion(3)` — but never calls `migrateToV3()`, so `idx_canonical` is never created.

## Impact

- All new user installations (v3 fresh installs) silently lack the index.
- `QueryByCanonical` doc comment says "The query uses idx_canonical for efficient lookup when family is non-empty" — this is false for fresh DBs.
- SQLite falls back to a full table scan; results remain correct but performance degrades as the models table grows.
- `TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed` only exercises the migration code path; there is no test for the fresh-DB path.

## Fix

Add `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider)` immediately after `sqlitex.ExecuteTransient(conn, schemaSQL, nil)` in the `!hasModels` branch of `migrateSchema()`. Alternatively, extract a shared `indexSQL` constant and call it from both the fresh-DB path and `migrateToV3`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:07:23Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:07:23Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-0eap","title":"IMPORTANT: TestMigration_V2Idempotent comment/name stale — test now creates v3 DB, not v2","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: B
  round: 1
  file: store_migration_test.go
  test: TestMigration_V2Idempotent
---
## Finding

TestMigration_V2Idempotent (store_migration_test.go, line ~229) has a stale comment:
  // First open — creates fresh v2 DB and writes a row.

But OpenStore() now always creates a v3 DB (currentSchemaVersion = 3), not a v2 DB.
The second open therefore tests v3 idempotency, not v2 idempotency.

Additionally the test name 'V2Idempotent' is misleading: it was not changed from the original
v2-era test despite the chain change in commit 9feddbd. A reader expects this test to exercise
a v2 DB opened a second time without re-migration; instead it tests that a v3 DB opened twice
stays at v3 — which is already covered by TestMigration_V2toV3_Idempotent.

There is now a gap: no test starts with a v2 DB on disk and opens it twice to verify idempotency
of the v2→v3 migration path (i.e., that running migration twice does not corrupt data).

## Suggested fix

Rename to TestMigration_V3Idempotent and update the comment, OR rewrite it to use createV2DB
so it genuinely tests v2 idempotency (first open: v2→v3 migration; second open: already v3, no re-migration).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:06:45Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:06:45Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-scy8","title":"IMPORTANT: IndexUsed test queries (family,variant,provider) but QueryByCanonical queries (family,variant,date)","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: B
  round: 1
  file: store_migration_test.go
  test: TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed
---
## Finding

TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed runs EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on:
  WHERE family = ?1 AND variant = ?2 AND provider = ?3

But QueryByCanonical (store.go) generates:
  WHERE family = ?1 [AND variant = ?2] [AND date = ?3]

idx_canonical is defined on (family, variant, provider). It does NOT include the date column.

The IndexUsed test proves idx_canonical exists and SQLite uses it for a query on (family, variant, provider). But it does NOT prove that QueryByCanonical itself uses the index. To verify QueryByCanonical uses the index, the EXPLAIN must reflect the actual query shape with a date predicate, not a provider predicate.

Note: SQLite will still use the (family, variant) prefix of idx_canonical for QueryByCanonical queries; date becomes a residual filter. But the test should assert on the actual query generated by QueryByCanonical, not a manually written approximation.

## Suggested fix

Change the EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN in TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed to match QueryByCanonical's actual predicate:
  WHERE family = ?1 AND variant = ?2 AND date = ?3
This ensures the test remains aligned with implementation as the query evolves.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:06:32Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:06:32Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-az7v","title":"IMPORTANT: Extract tableRecreate helper to eliminate migrateToV2/V3 duplication","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
migrateToV2 and migrateToV3 share a structurally identical 3-step table-recreate pattern (CREATE models_new → DROP models → RENAME models_new TO models) with identical error-format strings. As migrations accumulate (v4, v5…) this becomes a copy-paste hazard. Recommend extracting:

  func tableRecreate(conn *sqlite.Conn, createSQL, copySQL string) error

before the next migration is added. Both existing migrations remain self-contained and tested so this is not an immediate correctness issue.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:04:10Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:04:10Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-f8ea","title":"IMPORTANT: QueryByCanonical positional-string ambiguity — consider CanonicalFilter struct","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: reviewer-C
  round: 1
---
QueryByCanonical(ctx, family Family, variant, date string) has two trailing bare strings. At call sites, swapping variant and date is an undetectable positional error (both are untyped strings). Recommend:

  type CanonicalFilter struct { Family Family; Variant, Date string }
  func (s *Store) QueryByCanonical(ctx context.Context, f CanonicalFilter) ([]ModelInfo, error)

This makes the empty-wildcard contract self-documenting and keeps the signature extensible for future canonical axes (e.g., epoch) without a breaking change.

Not a blocker because internal usage surface is small and current doc comment is clear.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:04:19Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:04:19Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-a79x","title":"MINOR: IndexUsed test verifies provider-filter query, not the actual date-filter QueryByCanonical pattern","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-est
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
---
## Finding

`TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed` runs EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN with:
```sql
WHERE family = ?1 AND variant = ?2 AND provider = ?3
```

But `QueryByCanonical` actually queries:
```sql
WHERE family = ?1 AND variant = ?2 AND date = ?3
```

The index is `idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider)` — `date` is not a column in the index. SQLite will use the index for `family` and `variant` lookups but will need to scan for `date` filtering beyond those columns.

The test proves the index is used for a `provider`-filtered query, which correctly covers the cross-provider use case. However it does not verify the behavior for the primary API function's actual query pattern.

## Impact

Low — results are correct regardless. The index still accelerates family+variant lookups in `QueryByCanonical`. The `date` predicate filters residually after the index range scan.

## Fix (optional)

Add a secondary EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN assertion in `TestMigration_V2toV3_IndexUsed` (or a new sub-test) that runs the exact SQL shape issued by `QueryByCanonical` (i.e., filtering on date, not provider) and verifies idx_canonical is still referenced for the family+variant prefix.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:07:39Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:07:39Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-fh27","title":"MINOR: Backfill test has single row, missing release_date-takes-precedence branch for ExtractDate","description":"---

extraction). This validates that the backfill correctly threads release_date into ExtractDate.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:06:56Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:06:56Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-olgy","title":"MINOR: PreservesData test does not assert NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date after migration","description":"---

TestMigration_V2toV3_PreservesData checks that both rows survive migration and that
m.Family (raw_family) is preserved. It does NOT assert that NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant,
and NormalizedDate were backfilled for those rows.

This means PreservesData only tests structural survival (row count, raw_family identity),
not semantic correctness (canonical fields populated). The Backfill test covers this for
a single row, but PreservesData would be more complete if it also verified canonical fields
for the rows it inserts — since it uses model IDs like 'claude-opus-4-20250514' which have
deterministic parse outputs.

## Suggested fix

Add assertions for NormalizedFamily='claude', NormalizedVariant='opus', NormalizedDate='2025-05-14'
for the claude-opus-4-20250514 row in PreservesData, and NormalizedFamily='gemini-pro' (or whatever
ParseFamily('gemini-pro') returns), NormalizedVariant, NormalizedDate for the gemini-pro row.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:07:07Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:07:07Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-0ci9","title":"MINOR: testModel() fixture does not include NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date; store/merge round-trips omit normalized fields","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4

---
## Finding

The shared testModel() helper in store_test.go constructs ModelInfo with zero values for NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, and NormalizedDate. All store round-trip tests (insert → read back), merge tests, and the schema_test.go fixture also leave these three new fields unset.

Consequence: no automated test currently proves that:
- Store.Save() + Store.All() round-trips NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date correctly
- MergeModels() preserves normalized fields under most-recent-wins semantics
- FormatModel JSON output includes normalized fields with non-zero values

schema_test.go step-5/6 would catch a missing JSON key only if the fixture had non-zero values. As it stands the test accepts empty string ('') for all three fields, which satisfies the schema property check but doesn't prove the fields contain useful data.

## Why MINOR (not IMPORTANT)
The schema JSON property conformance check still passes. The fields are zero-value but present. This is a test hygiene gap rather than a correctness risk.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:39:49Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:39:49Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-irak","title":"MINOR: isExactIDMatch variable name slightly asymmetric with isExactIDInput helper name","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-99m
  reviewer: C
  round: 2
  file: resolve.go
  lines: '95, 206'
---
The function is named isExactIDInput (describing the input property) but the variable at the call site is isExactIDMatch (describing the result property). Both names are correct English and the intent is unambiguous. The doc comment on the variable (line 93-94) bridges the naming gap. However, a reader skimming resolve.go sees isExactIDMatch := ... isExactIDInput(...) and has to pause to reconcile the two names.

Suggestion: Rename the variable to isExactIDInput (matching the function name) — i.e., isExactIDInput := scheme == SchemeCanonical && isExactIDInput(...). Go permits shadowing the function name with a local bool. Or keep as-is; the cognitive cost is low.

Severity: MINOR — cosmetic only, no correctness impact.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:20:24Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:20:24Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-haiu","title":"MINOR: SchemeCanonical auto-detect rule silently treats any single-slash string as SchemeHuggingFace","description":"MINOR finding. Slice: bestiary-99m. Reviewer C round 1. Severity group: bestiary-35qs.

The detectScheme() heuristic (resolve.go): 'word/word' with exactly one slash and no pkg: prefix is treated as SchemeHuggingFace. This is fragile in a predictable way: a canonical triple like 'anthropic/claude' (which has exactly one slash) will be detected as HuggingFace and strip the provider prefix, giving matchInput='claude', not 'anthropic/claude'. The canonical family-lookup path is then never reached via auto-detect for two-segment canonical inputs — only via WithScheme(SchemeCanonical) explicitly.

This is not a production BLOCKER because the spec says SchemeCanonical is not an auto-detected scheme, but the comment in WithScheme's doc says '<word>/<word> → HF' which is accurate. The fragility is that a user typing 'anthropic/claude' into the CLI expecting canonical resolution will silently get HuggingFace behaviour. The resolve_test.go TestResolve_AutoDetect_HuggingFaceForm tests only that it works, not that the boundary is documented.

How to address (SLICE-6 or follow-up): Document in the Resolve godoc that auto-detect intentionally excludes SchemeCanonical (it must be explicit). Add a test case that asserts 'provider/family' is auto-detected as HuggingFace, not canonical. This is low-risk since SLICE-6 owns the CLI integration.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:08:45Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:08:45Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-ovn0","title":"MINOR: formatCanonical() uses string(r.Family) == empty check instead of r.Family == empty Family","description":"MINOR finding. Slice: bestiary-99m. Reviewer C round 1. Severity group: bestiary-35qs.\n\nmodelref.go line 70: 'if string(r.Family) == \"\"' converts Family (a named string type) to string before comparing. The idiomatic Go form is 'if r.Family == \"\"' (Go allows == comparison between a named string type and an untyped string literal). The conversion is harmless but slightly inconsistent with how other parts of the codebase use Family values. In the same file, SchemeHuggingFace and SchemePURL branches use string(r.ID) which is correct since ModelID is also a named string type and fmt.Sprintf accepts it either way. The Family comparison in formatCanonical should use r.Family == \"\" for consistency and to avoid future confusion about whether the type conversion is meaningful.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:08:52Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:08:52Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-v1jf","title":"IMPORTANT: YAML serializer missing NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date fields","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-hf4
  reviewer: reviewer-A
  round: 1
  file: format.go
---
## Finding

The custom YAML serializer in format.go (modelToYAML, lines 141-166) does not emit NormalizedFamily, NormalizedVariant, or NormalizedDate fields. The JSON path (json.MarshalIndent) emits them automatically because they are exported struct fields.

This creates a format divergence: users running `bestiary show --format yaml` get a model record missing the three new normalization fields, while `bestiary show --format json` includes them.

## Impact

End-users using YAML output (e.g., for config files or shell scripting) would see incomplete normalization data. They would not be able to rely on YAML output for canonical family/variant/date lookups.

No test catches this divergence — the YAML format tests do not assert NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date are present.

## Fix suggestion

Add three writeYAMLString calls to modelToYAML after the existing Family line:
```go
writeYAMLString(&sb, indent, "Family", string(m.Family))
writeYAMLString(&sb, indent, "NormalizedFamily", string(m.NormalizedFamily))
writeYAMLString(&sb, indent, "NormalizedVariant", m.NormalizedVariant)
writeYAMLString(&sb, indent, "NormalizedDate", m.NormalizedDate)
```
Add corresponding test assertions in format_test.go TestFormatModels_YAML or TestFormatModel_YAML.

This fix is within SLICE-2b scope since it introduced the fields and format.go is a shared file.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:41:55Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:41:55Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-6xdw","title":"IMPORTANT: TestFamily_RoundTrip does not include an unknown Family value","description":"---

  reviewer: B (Test Quality)

## Finding

TestFamily_RoundTrip only iterates over 6 known Family constants. The permissive contract — that unknown values also round-trip identically through MarshalText → UnmarshalText — is not directly verified in that test.

TestFamily_UnmarshalUnknownAccepted covers the unmarshal direction for unknowns, but the MarshalText side for an unknown value is not exercised explicitly.

## Why it matters

If MarshalText were ever changed to reject unknown values (e.g. return an error), TestFamily_RoundTrip would not catch the regression. The two tests together give incomplete coverage of the cross-product.

## Suggested fix

Add one or two unknown values to the RoundTrip families slice, e.g.:

    bestiary.Family(""),

This closes the gap without requiring a new test.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:35:09Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:35:09Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-fc2e","title":"MINOR: TestFamily_RoundTrip lacks t.Run subtests for per-value failure attribution","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-i047
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 1
  file: family_test.go
  function: TestFamily_RoundTrip
---
## Finding

TestFamily_RoundTrip iterates over a slice with a for-loop and uses `continue` on error. While `continue` prevents early exit, the test output on failure will only show the failing value as part of the error message string — not as a named subtest. Contrast with TestFamily_IsKnown which uses t.Run per group.

## Why it matters

Minor readability/debuggability issue. When a round-trip fails, the developer must read the error message to find which value failed rather than seeing a named subtest in the -v output.

## Suggested fix

Wrap each iteration body in t.Run(string(f), func(t *testing.T) { ... }) for richer test output and proper t.Fatal/t.Error isolation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:35:22Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:35:22Z"}

TestParseScheme_ErrorIsActionable (canonical_test.go) checks that err != nil and msg != "", then calls errors.Unwrap(err) just to verify it doesn't panic. {"id":"bestiary-7ef0","title":"MINOR: TestResolve_WithSchemeHuggingFace silently skips assertion on ErrNotFound","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-99m
  reviewer: reviewer-B
  round: 1
---
TestResolve_WithSchemeHuggingFace in resolve_test.go uses a model ID ('openai/gpt-4o-2024-08-06') that may or may not be in the static dataset. When the model is absent the test returns early with 'return' after confirming the error is not ErrAmbiguous — it does NOT verify that the error is ErrNotFound.

This means the contract 'SchemeHuggingFace with an absent ID returns ErrNotFound, not ErrAmbiguous' is only half-asserted. A regression that returns a generic error.New("not found") string would still pass.

Suggestion: when the lookup fails, add: if !errors.As(err, new(*bestiary.ErrNotFound)) { t.Errorf(...) }. Alternatively, use a model ID that IS guaranteed to be in the static registry (e.g., anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514, which the cross-provider test already validates).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:10:12Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:10:12Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-7i4c","title":"IMPORTANT: TestModelConstants_NamingStable does not test codegen idempotency","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ktj
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 1
---
## Finding

TestModelConstants_NamingStable is named and documented as testing "naming stability" and "idempotency" of codegen, but it does not re-run `go generate ./...` and compare the output. It only checks (a) defensive copy mutation and (b) that returned values don't start with "Model_". The spec checklist item from Reviewer B-F4 asks for "re-run codegen and assert same names".

## Impact

A regression where codegen produces different names on the second run (non-deterministic ordering, changed tokenizer) would NOT be caught by this test.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Rename the test to TestModelIDs_ValuesAreRawIDs (which is what it actually tests), OR
2. Add a separate TestModelConstants_NamingStable that writes current generated source to a temp file, re-runs generateConstantsSource() on the same model set, and asserts byte-identical output.

Option 2 is the more complete path. Option 1 is acceptable if codegen idempotency is considered out-of-scope for runtime tests (since the generated file is committed, a CI re-generate-and-diff check would cover it).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:12:37Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:12:37Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-b20n","title":"IMPORTANT: TestModelConstants_Unique threshold too loose (1000 vs 4327 actual)","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-ktj
  reviewer: B (Test Quality)
  round: 1
---
## Finding

TestModelConstants_Unique uses `const minExpected = 1000` as its sanity-check floor, but the generated file currently contains 4327 constants. A regression that deletes 80% of the constants would still pass this test.

## Impact

Large silent regressions in constant coverage (e.g. skip-rule applied too aggressively, family normalization broken) would go undetected.

## Suggested Fix

Set the floor to something proportional to the known current output — e.g. 3000 — and add a comment explaining the basis: "as of YYYY-MM-DD the generated count is N; this floor is set at ~70% of that to tolerate upstream model list churn without false failures".","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:12:45Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:12:45Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-0ip","title":"IMPL_PLAN: Entity normalization pipeline","description":"---
references:
  request: bestiary-n6x
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  elicit: bestiary-73x
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  handoff: bestiary-28c
  uat: bestiary-qz7
---

## Objective
Decompose the ratified PROPOSAL-3 (bestiary-1oq) into vertical slices with explicit horizontal Layer Integration Points. Each slice owns a full vertical (types → tests → impl → wiring) and is implemented by exactly one worker.

## Decomposition: 8 Vertical Slices

### Slice 1 — parse package + codegen template apply (foundation)
Owner files (NEW): `parse.go`, `parse_test.go`, `parse/data/family_overrides.json`, `parse/data/variant_suffixes.json`, `parse/data/version_patterns.json`.
Touched: `families_gen.go`, `models_static_gen.go`, `providers_gen.go` (regenerate via `go generate ./...` to apply commit 6a8d75b template fix — must end with `type Family string`, NOT `type Family = string`). `.bestiary-gen-cache/api_response.json` (dirty, will be re-cached).

Deliverables:
- `ParseFamily(raw Family) (Family, string)` — uses overrides table → suffix table → versioned-variant patterns (v/k/m/no-prefix). Hyphen-separated versions (claude-opus-4-5) handled. Empty raw families return zero values.
- `ExtractDate(id ModelID, releaseDate string) string` — match YYYYMMDD or YYYY-MM-DD only.
- `inferFamilyFromID(id ModelID, p Provider) Family` — fallback for empty-family models (~25%).
- Embed JSON via `//go:embed parse/data/*.json` (regular package directory, NOT testdata/).
- `go generate ./...` regenerates all `_gen.go` files with named Family type.

### Slice 2 — ModelInfo extension + family methods + codegen normalization splice
Owner files (NEW): `family.go`, `family_test.go`.
Touched: `bestiary.go` (ModelInfo new fields), `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (genToModelInfo at line 524, validateGeneratedFamilyType post-condition).

Deliverables:
- ModelInfo gains `NormalizedFamily Family`, `NormalizedVariant string`, `NormalizedDate string`.
- `family.go`: `Family.IsKnown()`, `String()`, `MarshalText()`, `UnmarshalText()` — resolves debt task bestiary-ptm.
- Codegen splice: invoke `parse.ParseFamily` + `parse.ExtractDate` in `genToModelInfo()` to bake Normalized fields into `models_static_gen.go`.
- `validateGeneratedFamilyType(path)` post-condition runs at end of `run()` and asserts grep for `type Family string` (no `=`).
- TestFamilyType_NamedNotAlias regression test in package root.

### Slice 3 — ModelRef + canonical + designation + Resolve (public API)
Owner files (NEW): `canonical.go`, `designation.go`, `resolve.go`, `canonical_test.go`, `designation_test.go`, `resolve_test.go`.
Touched: `modelref.go` (ModelRef gains ID field, becomes 6-field; Format(scheme) dispatch; Designations() method; Stringer uses SchemeCanonical), `errors.go` (NEW *ErrAmbiguous struct error), `errors_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- `CanonicalScheme` int enum: SchemeCanonical | SchemeHuggingFace | SchemePURL | SchemeRaw (UAT Option A formats).
- `Designation{Value, Scheme, Provider, Rating}`, `AcceptabilityRating` int enum (Admitted | Preferred | Deprecated). All static designations default to Admitted.
- ModelRef 6 fields: ID, Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Date.
- ModelRef.Format(scheme), ModelRef.Designations().
- `Resolve(input string, opts ...ResolveOption) ([]ModelRef, error)` — slice for cross-provider hosting, *ErrAmbiguous for distinct canonicals, ErrNotFound for zero. WithScheme(s) option.

### Slice 4 — Model_ constants generation
Owner files (NEW): `cmd/bestiary-gen` additions for `generateConstantsSource()`, `models_constants_gen.go` (generated), `constants_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- New codegen step: emits `Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Date>?` constants. Underscore-separated component naming. Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase.
- Two-pass collision algorithm: append disambiguator from raw ID version segment, fall back to `_<n>` sequential suffix.
- Skip rule: empty family AND no extractable family from ID → no constant generated.
- `Models() []ModelID` returns all generated constants (defensive copy).
- Tests: TestModelConstants_Unique, TestModelConstants_RoundTrip, TestModelConstants_NamingStable.

### Slice 5 — SQLite v2→v3 migration + QueryByCanonical
Owner files (NEW): `migrations/v2_to_v3.sql`.
Touched: `store.go` (migrateToV3 mirroring migrateToV2 pattern, currentSchemaVersion bump, scanModelInfo + UpsertModels column-list updates, new QueryByCanonical method), `store_migration_test.go`, `store_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- v2→v3 migration: rename existing `family` → `raw_family`; add `family` (parsed), `variant`, `date` columns; create `idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider)`.
- Backfill on first migrate: re-run parse logic on existing rows.
- QueryByCanonical(ctx, family, variant, date) ([]ModelInfo, error).
- Standalone SQL file at `migrations/v2_to_v3.sql` containing equivalent ALTER/CREATE statements (reference doc, not executed by Go code).
- Migration test: 5 sub-tests — preserves-data, backfill, idempotent, edge-cases, index-used.

### Slice 6 — CLI extensions (bestiary show + bestiary-gen cache flags)
Touched: `cmd/bestiary/main.go` (show: --scheme=<canonical|huggingface|purl|raw> flag, auto-detect by prefix, ErrAmbiguous candidate-list rendering), `cmd/bestiary/main_test.go`, `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (--cache-dir <path> and --no-fetch flags), `cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go`.

{"id":"bestiary-gkpx","title":"SLICE-FIX-1: Add Version field to ModelRef + parser + cascades","description":"---
